customers.relationship: customer and partner for Xbox and cloud services
customers.context: Under our agreement with Microsoft for the Xbox, if someone makes an offer to purchase at least 30% of our outstanding common stock, Microsoft may have first and last rights of refusal to purchase the stock.

geographic_concentration.context: While currently our supply chain is mainly concentrated in Asia, we are expanding into the U.S. and Latin America. Our semiconductor wafers are manufactured, assembled, tested and packaged by third parties located outside of the United States.
Finally, our business depends on our ability to receive consistent and reliable supply from our overseas partners, especially in Taiwan and South Korea.

capacity_constraints.context: In periods of shortages impacting the semiconductor industry and/or limited supply or capacity in our supply chain, the lead times for certain supply may be extended. We have previously experienced and may continue to experience extended lead times of more than 12 months.
We expect supply constraints to be a headwind to Gaming in the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 and beyond.

supply_chain_risks.context: Over the past three years, we have been subject to a series of shifting and expanding export control restrictions, impacting our ability to serve customers outside the United States.
Geopolitical tensions in regions where we rely on suppliers, contract manufacturers, and assembly partners that are critical to our supply continuity, could have a material adverse impact on us.
As a result, any H200 shipped under the new licensing program will be subject to a 25% tariff upon importation into the United States.

revenue_concentration.context: For fiscal year 2026, sales to one direct customer represented 22% of total revenue... primarily attributable to the Compute & Networking segment.
sales to another direct customer represented 14% of total revenue, all of which were primarily attributable to the Compute & Networking segment.

geographic_revenue.context: United States revenue was $149,617 million for the year ended Jan 25, 2026.
Taiwan revenue was $42,345 million for the year ended Jan 25, 2026.
China (including Hong Kong) revenue was $19,677 million for the year ended Jan 25, 2026.

purchase_obligations.context: As of January 25, 2026, these commitments were $95.2 billion, of which substantially all will be paid through fiscal year 2027.

Multi-year cloud service agreement commitments as of January 25, 2026, were $27 billion, for which $7 billion, $6 billion, $5 billion, $5 billion, $2 billion, and $2 billion will be paid in fiscal years 2027, 2028, 2029, 2030, 2031, and 2032 and thereafter, respectively.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: fixed-rate investment portfolio and outstanding debt
market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: 0.5% decrease in yield curve = $0.2 billion decrease in fair value
market_risk_disclosures.context: According to our sensitivity analysis on our investment portfolio, a decrease in the yield curve of 0.5% as of the end of fiscal year 2026 and 2025 would decrease the fair value for these investments by approximately $0.2 billion.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: foreign currency exchange rate movements on operating expenses
market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: 10% USD strengthening = $180 million adverse impact on AOCI
market_risk_disclosures.context: If the U.S. dollar strengthened by 10% as of January 25, 2026 and January 26, 2025, the amount recorded in Accumulated other comprehensive income (loss) related to our foreign exchange contracts before tax effect would have been an adverse impact of $180 million and $136 million, respectively.

inventory_composition.context: Raw materials inventory was $3,807 million as of January 25, 2026.
